note:
信奇首席产品官维克拉姆·坎德普尔(Vikram Khandpur)表示，根据地点的不同，业务通过不同的消息渠道进行，如短信、WhatsApp或Viber，企业必须能够从技术和地理角度与客户在任何地方进行沟通。更重要的是，当在线交流变得至关重要时，这种需求在大流行期间变得更加明显。
Khandpur表示，到目前为止，Sch一直专注于优化SMS体验，以实现客户获取、客户参与、送货通知和客户支持等行动。现在，该公司希望下一步进入更丰富的全渠道消息传递领域。
我们的想法是提供一套工具，通过引导营销团队完成每个参与者所需的流程，来帮助营销团队通过这些多个渠道进行沟通。“通过写信给我们的API，我们能提供的是，如果我们的客户还没有在这些平台上，我们可以在所有这些平台上获得客户，”他说。
他以WhatsApp为例，因为它有一个非常明确的流程，供品牌使用。“在WhatsApp上，有这样一个概念，就是创建这些预先批准的模板，它们需要经过WhatsApp团队的审查、策划和最终批准。我们在这个过程中帮助他们，然后在其他渠道和平台上做同样的事情，所以我们从品牌中去除了这种复杂性。“Khandpur解释说。
他补充说，“只要给我们一次这个信息，我们就会在需要的时候照顾到幕后的所有[不同的工具]进行代码转换。所以如果你用图片和视频给我们一个非常丰富的信息，WhatsApp可能想要用一种特定的方式来呈现它，但是Viber用一种不同的方式来呈现它，我们会注意到这一点。
营销人员可以使用对话API来定义参数，比如首先在印度使用WhatsApp，但是如果目标客户没有打开WhatsApp，那么就退回到SMS。
该公司去年进行了四次收购，包括印度的ACL Mobile和SAP的互连消息业务，以增强其在世界各地的影响力。
辛奇在通信API领域与Twilio竞争，它可能是你从未听说过的最成功的公司之一，去年创造了超过5亿美元的收入，处理了超过1100亿条消息。
该公司于2008年在瑞典成立，从未接受过一分钱的风险投资，但自早期以来一直处于盈利状态。事实上，该公司已在斯德哥尔摩的纳斯达克交易所公开交易，并将于下周公布财报。
note_en:
Sinch   chief product officer Vikram Khandpur says that business is being conducted in different messaging channels such as SMS, WhatsApp or Viber depending on location, and businesses have to be able to communicate with their customers wherever they happen to be from a technology and geographic standpoint. What’s more, this need has become even more pronounced during a pandemic when online communication has become paramount.
 Khandpur says that up until now, Sinch has concentrated on optimizing the SMS experience for actions like customer acquisition, customer engagement, delivery notifications and customer support. Now the company wants to take that next step into richer omni-channel messaging.
   The idea is to provide a set of tools to help marketing teams communicate across these multiple channels by walking them through the processes required by each player. “By writing to our API, what we can provide is that we can get our customers on all these platforms if they are not already on these platforms,” he said.
 He uses WhatsApp as an example because it has a very defined process for brands to work with it. “On WhatsApp, there is this concept of creating these pre-approved templates, and they need to be reviewed, curated and finally approved by the WhatsApp team. We help them with that process, and then do the same with other channels and platforms, so we take that complexity away from the brand,” Khandpur explained.
 He adds, “By giving us that message once, we take care of all of [the different tools] behind the scenes transcoding when needed. So if you give us a very rich message with images and videos, WhatsApp may want to render it in a certain way, but then Viber renders that in a different way, and we take care of that.
  Marketers can use the Conversation API to define parameters like using WhatsApp first in India, but if the targeted customer doesn’t open WhatsApp, then fall back to SMS.
 The company has made four acquisitions in the last year including  ACL Mobile in India and  SAP’s Interconnect Messaging business to enhance its presence across the world.
 Sinch, which competes with Twilio in the communications API space, may be one of the most successful companies you never heard of, generating over $500 million in revenue last year while processing over 110 billion messages.
 The company launched in Sweden in 2008 and has never taken a dime of venture capital, yet has been profitable since early days. In fact, it’s publicly traded on the NASDAQ Exchange in Stockholm and will be reporting earnings next week.
title: 辛奇宣布推出对话API，将多种消息传递工具整合在一起
title_en: Sinch announces Conversation API to bring together multiple messaging tools

这家总部位于渥太华的零售商电子商务服务提供商公布第三季度收益为1.332亿美元，合每股1.13美元，去年同期亏损3360万美元，合每股亏损29美分。跟踪该公司的分析师此前预计每股收益为52美分。
该公司业务的几乎一切看起来都很好，部分原因是实体零售额暴跌，因为限制新型冠状病毒传播的卫生法规，如入住率限制，已经减缓了客流量。
Shopify本季度营收为7.674亿美元，同比增长96%，轻松超过分析师预测的约6.58亿美元。营业收入也比去年同期有所上升，Shopify的营业收入约为5,000万美元，占收入的7%，而去年同期亏损近3,600万美元。调整后的营业收入接近1.31亿美元。
Shopify总裁哈雷·芬克尔斯坦(Harley Finkelstein)在一份声明中表示：“随着越来越多的消费者在网上购物，企业家们加快步伐以满足需求，新冠肺炎引发的向数字商务的加速转变仍在继续。”“企业家将成为重建全球经济的力量，这使得Shopify创新并打造商家在低接触零售环境中取得成功所需的关键工具变得更加重要.”
Shopify首席财务官艾米·夏佩罗(Amy Shapero)表示：“Shopify第三季度业绩惊人，反映了我们商家的韧性和创业精神。”越来越多的企业家正在签约Shopify，这样他们就可以快速、轻松地将自己的想法付诸实践。我们继续发展我们的全球商业运营系统，使商家更容易上网并开始销售，被发现，并将他们的商品送到买家手中，同时提供令人愉快的购物体验。“。
Shopify之所以有趣，不仅是因为它自己的收入，也是因为它的收入说明了直接面向消费者的零售业务的健康状况-其中一些业务已经从风险资本家那里筹集了大量投资。
看看该公司的商用解决方案收入，增长了132%，达到5.221亿美元--这些直接面向消费者的公司的利润状况肯定相当健康。Shopify从中获得商家解决方案收益的商品总交易量为300亿美元。这一数字比去年同期增加了161亿美元。
Shopify坐拥相当可观的财务缓冲，拥有61.2亿美元的现金和等价物，高于年初的24.6亿美元。
在金融业务之外，Shopify正在采取行动，通过最近与TikTok的合作，扩大其在社交商务领域的足迹。两家公司表示，这笔交易应该会让更多的Shopify卖家使用与Shopify仪表盘集成的工具包，直接在TikTok平台上进行营销，从而接触到TikTok的受众。
Shopify首席财务官艾米·夏佩罗(Amy Shapero)表示：“越来越多的企业家正在签约Shopify，这样他们就可以快速、轻松地将自己的想法付诸实践。”我们继续发展我们的全球商业运营系统，使商家更容易上网并开始销售，被发现，并将他们的商品送到买家手中，同时提供令人愉快的购物体验。“
note_en:
The Ottawa-based provider of e-commerce services for retailers reported earnings of $133.2 million, or $1.13 per-share, for the third quarter after posting a loss of $33.6 million, or 29 cents per-share, in the same period last year. Analysts tracking the company had expected earnings per-share of 52 cents.
   Pretty much everything about the company’s business looked good, partly driven by plummeting brick-and-mortar retail sales as health regulations to limit the spread of the novel coronavirus like occupancy constraints have slowed down foot traffic.
 Shopify’s $767.4 million in revenue for the quarter was up 96% from a year ago and handily beat the expectations of analysts who were predicting for the company to bring in roughly $658 million. Operating income was also up from the year-ago period with Shopify calling about $50 million, or 7% of revenue, compared to a nearly $36 million loss for the year ago period. Adjusted operating income was nearly $131 million.
 “The accelerated shift to digital commerce triggered by COVID-19 is continuing, as more consumers shop online and entrepreneurs step up to meet demand,” said Harley Finkelstein, Shopify’s President, in a statement. “Entrepreneurs will be the force in rebuilding economies all over the world, which makes it even more important for Shopify to innovate and build the critical tools that merchants need to succeed in a low-touch retail environment.”
 “Shopify’s tremendous third-quarter results reflect the resilience and entrepreneurial spirit of our merchants,” said Amy Shapero, Shopify’s  CFO  . “More entrepreneurs are signing on to Shopify so they can quickly and easily put their ideas into action. We continue to evolve our global commerce operating system to make it easier for merchants to get online and start selling, get discovered, and get their goods to buyers, while providing a delightful shopping experience.”
 Shopify is interesting not only for its own revenue, but what its revenues say about the health of direct-to-consumer retail businesses — some of which have raised significant investment from venture capitalists.
 Looking at the company’s merchant solutions revenue, which grew by 132% to $522.1 million — the state of these direct-to-consumer companies’ bottom line must be pretty healthy. Gross merchandise volume, the figure from which Shopify derives its merchant solutions gains, was $30 billion. That figure is an increase of $16.1 billion over the year-ago period.
 Shopify is sitting on a pretty hefty financial cushion with $6.12 billion in cash and equivalents, up from $2.46 billion at the start of the year.
 Outside its financials, Shopify is making moves to expand its footprint in social commerce,  through a recent partnership with TikTok, announced yesterday. The deal should enable more Shopify sellers to reach TikTok’s audience by marketing directly on the platform using a toolkit integrated with Shopify’s dashboard, the two companies said.
  “More entrepreneurs are signing on to Shopify so they can quickly and easily put their ideas into action,” said Amy Shapero, Shopify’s chief financial officer. “We continue to evolve our global commerce operating system to make it easier for merchants to get online and start selling, get discovered, and get their goods to buyers, while providing a delightful shopping experience.”
title: Shopify股价在盘前交易中上涨，收益超出预期
title_en: Shopify stock is up in pre-market trading as earnings blow past estimates

note:
Facebook因未能保护用户的个人数据而被起诉，该事件发生在剑桥分析公司(Cambridge Analytica)的泄密事件中。
这起丑闻涉及获取的Facebook数据，即8700万人在选举期间被用于广告。
针对Facebook滥用英格兰和威尔士近100万用户信息的大规模法律行动正在展开。
采取行动的组织-Facebook，你欠我们的-之前曾对谷歌提起过类似的大规模诉讼。
谷歌，你欠我们的，由前哪位领导？局长理查德·劳埃德(Richard Lloyd)也在积极调查另一起涉嫌大规模数据泄露的案件。
两人都由米尔伯格·伦敦律师事务所(Millberg London)代理，谷歌案将于明年4月在最高法院开庭审理。
Facebook的案件将辩称，该公司在未经同意的情况下获取数据，未能履行其根据1998年数据保护法(Data Protection Act)承担的法律义务。
我们还没有收到任何关于这项索赔的文件。Facebook的一位发言人表示，信息专员办公室对这些问题的调查，包括扣押和讯问剑桥分析公司的服务器，没有发现任何证据表明科根博士将任何英国或欧盟用户的数据转移到了剑桥分析公司。
2018年10月，英国数据保护监管机构对Facebook GB处以50万英镑的罚款，原因是该公司在剑桥分析(Cambridge Analytica)丑闻中扮演的角色。
信息专员办公室(ICO)表示，Facebook允许严重违反法律。
虽然如此大规模的法律诉讼在英国没有先例，但在美国却有。
2012年，在美国联邦贸易委员会(FTC)就同一问题提起的一起案件中，谷歌同意支付创纪录的2250万美元(1680万英镑)。
本案的代表索赔人阿尔文·卡皮奥(Alvin Carpio)表示：当我们使用Facebook时，我们希望我们的个人数据被负责任、透明和合法地使用。
由于未能保护我们的个人信息不被滥用，我们认为Facebook触犯了法律。
支付不到你年收入0.01%的罚款-给Facebook的零花钱-显然是一种不符合犯罪的惩罚。
note_en:
Facebook is being sued for failing to protect users' personal data in the Cambridge Analytica breach.
 The scandal involved harvested Facebook data of 87 million people being used for advertising during elections.
 Mass legal action is being launched against Facebook for misuse of information from almost one million users in England and Wales.
  The group taking action - Facebook You Owe Us - follows a similar mass action law suit against Google.
 Google You Owe Us, led by former Which? director Richard Lloyd, is also active for another alleged mass data breach.
 Both represented by law firm Millberg London, the Google case is being heard in the Supreme Court in April next year.
 The Facebook case will argue that by taking data without consent, the firm failed to meet their legal obligations under the Data Protection Act 1998.
 "We have not received any documents regarding this claim. The Information Commissioner's Office investigation into these issues, which included seizing and interrogating Cambridge Analytica's servers, found no evidence that any UK or EU users' data was transferred by Dr Kogan to Cambridge Analytica," a Facebook company spokesperson said.
  In October 2018, the UK's data protection watchdog  fined Facebook £500,000 for its role in the Cambridge Analytica scandal.
 The Information Commissioner's Office (ICO) said Facebook had allowed a "serious breach" of the law.
  Although there is no precedent for such a mass legal action in the UK, there is in the US.
 Google agreed to pay a record $22.5m (£16.8m) in a case brought by the US Federal Trade Commission (FTC) on the same issue in 2012.
   Representative claimant in the case Alvin Carpio said: "When we use Facebook, we expect that our personal data is being used responsibly, transparently, and legally.
 "By failing to protect our personal information from abuse, we believe that Facebook broke the law.
 "Paying less than 0.01% of your annual revenue in fines - pocket change to Facebook - is clearly a punishment that does not fit the crime.
title: Facebook因剑桥分析公司丑闻成为英国法律行动的目标
title_en: Facebook Targeted In UK Legal Action Over Cambridge Analytica Scandal

note:
总部位于阿姆斯特丹的CodeSandbox已经筹集了1270万美元，用于帮助团队合作开发基于网络的应用程序。随着团队变得更加分散，开发周期加快，该公司看到了通过让非开发人员更容易参与来促进创意过程的机会。
EQT Ventures领导了这轮融资，之前的投资者参与其中，包括凯鹏华盈(Kleiner Perkins)、Arches Capital和天使投资者吉列尔莫·劳赫(Guillermo Rauch)。该公司现在已经总共筹集了1500万美元，计划用这笔钱推出Pro Workspace，并将其令人惊讶的增长货币化。
“我们希望扩大CodeSandbox的使用案例，”首席执行官兼联合创始人艾夫斯·范·霍恩(Ives Van Hoorne)说。“我们希望让产品团队中的任何人都能为最终结果做出贡献.”
CodeSandbox是一个更广泛的举措的一部分，目的是简化编码并使其更容易访问-通常被称为“低码”或“无码”。这是一个大趋势，这意味着CodeSandbox也将面临一些强有力的竞争对手，比如Airtable，它最近以25.85亿美元的估值筹集了1.85亿美元。在协作方面，GitHub的Atom文本编辑器可以让程序员协同工作。
CodeSandbox由Van Horne和Bas Buursma于2017年创立，通过创建基于浏览器的服务在协作和低代码元素之间架起了桥梁。这个概念是范霍恩几年前度假时想到的。团队成员不停地问他问题，但他只带了一部手机，无法访问开发环境。
虽然有针对单个程序员的基于Web的工具，但CodeSandbox正在通过向团队提供项目来扩展这一想法。
此外，编码项目的呈现方式允许可能没有编码技能的产品设计人员和经理直接进行或接受更改。这避免了将建议传递给开发人员、等待更改并在进入下一步之前对其进行审查的繁琐过程。
联合创始人开始了这项服务，作为一个附带项目。直到两年前使用量开始激增后，他们才决定将其转变为一家官方公司。CodeSandbox的每月用户超过250万，在该平台上总共创建了超过1000万个项目。
虽然CodeSandbox到目前为止一直专注于协作，但即将推出的Pro Workspace产品将引入一些功能，让非开发人员可以访问项目。
与此同时，EQT的投资是其人工智能驱动的MotherBrain工具推动的第八笔交易。正在处理的数据突显了CodeSandbox的早期种子投资，以及其快速增长的用户群等因素，促使合作伙伴在该公司不打算融资的时候与其接触。
在接受EQT采访时，范·霍恩表示，他很惊讶该公司的合伙人似乎对CodeSandbox的业务了解如此之多。当该公司最终决定再次融资时，EQT位居榜首。
EQT分析合伙人Henrik Landgren说：“我们很高兴看到我们的CodeSandbox数据驱动的主动方法取得了成果。”“我们很早就看到了这家公司，研究了这个空间，跟踪了他们，然后决定联系他们。MotherBrain还帮助我们找到并比较了其他各种相关公司，这让我们对CodeSandbox更加兴奋。“
note_en:
Amsterdam-based  CodeSandbox has raised $12.7 million to help teams collaborate on the development of web-based applications. With teams becoming more distributed and development cycles accelerating, the company sees an opportunity to facilitate the creative process by making it easy for non-developers to participate.
 EQT Ventures led the round, with participation from previous investors, including Kleiner Perkins, Arches Capital, and angel investor Guillermo Rauch. The company, which has now raised a total of $15 million, plans to use the money to launch Pro Workspaces and monetize its surprising growth.
  “We want to expand our use cases for CodeSandbox,” CEO and cofounder Ives van Hoorne said. “We want to make it possible for anyone on the product team to contribute to the final results.”
 CodeSandbox is part of a broader move to simplify coding and make it more accessible — commonly referred to as “low code” or “no code.” It’s a big trend, which means CodeSandbox will also face some tough competitors, like  Airtable, which recently  raised $185 million at a $2.585 billion valuatio n. On the collaboration side,  GitHub’s Atom text editor lets coders work together.
 Founded by van Horne and Bas Buursma in 2017, CodeSandbox bridges the collaboration and low-code elements by creating a browser-based service. The concept occurred to van Horne a few years ago while he was on vacation. Team members kept asking him questions, but he only had a phone with him and couldn’t access the development environment.
 While there are web-based tools for individual coders, CodeSandbox is expanding this idea by making projects available to teams.
 In addition, coding projects are presented in a way that allows product designers and managers, who may not have coding skills, to directly make or accept changes. This avoids the cumbersome process of passing suggestions to developers, waiting for changes, and reviewing them before moving to the next step.
  The cofounders started the service as a side project. It was only after usage began to surge two years ago that they decided to turn it into an official company. CodeSandbox’s more than 2.5 million monthly users have together created over 10 million projects on the platform.
 While CodeSandbox has so far focused on collaboration, the upcoming Pro Workspaces product will introduce features that make projects accessible to non-developers.
 Meanwhile, EQT’s investment was the eighth deal prompted by its  AI-driven Motherbrain tool. The data being crunched highlighted CodeSandbox’s early seed investment, as well as factors like its rapidly growing userbase, prompting partners to reach out to the company when it wasn’t looking to raise money.
 In talking with EQT, van Hoorne said he was surprised how much the firm’s partners seemed to know about CodeSandbox’s business. When the company finally did decide to raise another round, EQT was at the top of the list.
 “We were happy to see the fruits from our data-driven proactive approach with CodeSandbox,” EQT analytics partner Henrik Landgren said. “We saw this company early on, researched the space, tracked them, and then decided to reach out. Motherbrain also helped us to find and compare the various other related companies, which made us even more excited about CodeSandbox.”
title: CodeSandbox是一家为协作应用程序开发构建基于网络的工具的公司，在EQT Ventures的带领下筹集了1270万美元，使其融资总额达到1500万美元
title_en: CodeSandbox, which builds web-based tools for collaborative application development, raises $12.7M led by EQT Ventures, bringing its total raised to $15M

note:
序列中的每个术语都是前两个术语的总和。也就是说，2=1+1，3=2+1，5=2+3，依此类推。斐波那契系列出现在自然界的许多地方，例如，向日葵种子的生长模式，以及许多植物的叶子模式。1/1，2/1，3/2，5/3...。这个数字被希腊人称为黄金比率。这个数字还表现出深思熟虑的性质，描述了贝壳的螺旋行为，此外，这个数字出现在许多数学环境中，与五边形和对数螺旋有关，举几个例子。斐波纳奇级数本身就有许多奇特而奇特的性质，比如我们即将描述的关于1/89的性质。事实上，有如此之多的这种奇怪的相互关系，有一本名为“斐波纳契季刊”的数学期刊，完全致力于系列及其性质。
1/89的小数展开式就是斐波纳契级数，以适当的方式相加在一起。具体地说，把斐波纳契数列看作是一个小数序列，排列成第n+1个小数点，第n个斐波纳契数的最右边的数字就是这样排列的。然后添加：0.01.001.0002.00003.000005.0000008.00000013.000000021.0000000034.00000000055.000000000089.0000000000144。.+.-.01123595505...。

Each term in the sequence is the sum of the previous two. Thatis, 2 = 1 + 1, 3 = 2 + 1, 5 = 2 + 3, and so on. The Fibonacci series appears in a number of places throughout nature,for example, in the pattern in which sunflower seeds grow, and the leafpatterns of many plants. The successive ratios of the terms, i.e. 1/1, 2/1, 3/2, 5/3 ... tendto a number called the Golden Ratio by the Greeks. This number also appearsthoughout nature, describing the spiral behavior of shells among other things.In addition, this number appears in many mathematical contexts, being relatedto pentagons and logarithmic spirals, to name a couple. The Fibbonaci series itself has many bizarre and curious properties, such as the one concerning 1/89 we are about to describe. In fact, thereare so many of these strange interrelations, there is a mathematical journalcalled the Fibonacci Quarterly, entirely devoted to the series and itsproperties.
 The decimal expansion of 1/89 is just the Fibonacci series, addedtogether in an appropriate fashion.  Specifically, think of the Fibonacci series as being a sequence ofdecimal fractions, arranged so the right most digit of the  nthFibonacci number is in the  n+1th decimal place. 上周，我的一个同龄人让我解释一下我所说的数据直觉是什么意思，我意识到我真的没有一个好的定义。这是个问题！我一直在说数据直觉！
数据直觉是我面试新数据科学家的三项技能之一(还有统计学和技术技能)。事实上，我刚刚花了2020年的前九个月建立了Mozilla的数据直觉。我真的很惊讶地意识到，我无法很好地解释我正在努力培养的东西。
换句话说，如果某人有很强的数据直觉，那么用数据来误导他们就更难了，可以把这看作是对黑暗数据艺术的一种防御。
数据直觉很强的人可以很快发现数据臭味(代码气味的近亲)。这些数据问题不一定会使分析无效，但肯定会引起对结果的怀疑。例如：
一项分析突出地报告了一个看似武断的指标-4天保留率增加了0.5%！4天保留期是从哪里来的？我们不是通常跟踪7天的保留期吗？在我相信结果之前，这需要更多的关注。
在预期名义结果的情况下，一项分析报告了非凡的结果-此功能将保留率提高了10%！但是，过去的努力是试图将留存率提高0.5%，而留存率不是已经达到90%了吗？我们是如何获得并增长10%的呢？
这些都是极端的例子。通常情况下，问题更微妙，会导致人们对结果普遍感到不安(这就是为什么它被称为直觉)。
我很清楚，数据直觉与产品直觉是相关的，尽管它们是不同的技能。产品直觉可以将我们的结果联系起来，让我们更容易识别分析中的特殊主张。要知道留存率提高10%是荒谬的，我们需要知道用户已经保持得很好了。
强大的数据直觉还可以帮助您发现分析设计方面的问题。比如：作者是如何收集数据的？这是一个有代表性的样本吗？他们需要进行实验才能确定因果关系吗？
这里有一个例子--比如一项分析报告说，创建Firefox账户的Firefox用户比没有创建Firefox账户的用户高出10%。默认情况下，许多人将其解读为，如果我们投入一些时间帮助用户开户，我们将看到留存率增加。相反，数据直觉较强的人会意识到这些结果只是相关的(而不是因果的)。
经常使用该产品的用户往往停留的时间更长。开户的用户是更活跃的用户，因此他们保留得更好。使Firefox崩溃的用户是更活跃的用户，也保留得更好。
我认为这种直觉不仅仅是对统计学的理解。强大的统计背景可以帮助我在阅读白皮书的方法部分时发现问题。强大的数据直觉帮助我确定我在多大程度上信任我在新闻标题中听到的结果。数据直觉帮助我确定结果是否足够真实。
我几乎将数据直觉定义为一种怀疑主义，但我认为这是一种糟糕的描述。怀疑主义过分关注于忽视结果。
直觉不仅仅是怀疑。它将新数据作为现有知识体系的一部分。很多时候，这意味着认定新的传入数据是不一致的，需要更多的调查才能信任它们。但在其他时候，这意味着面对比我们现有知识体系更权威的新数据，这意味着改变我们的观点。
我想听听你们对这个问题的看法。我之所以公开发布这个定义，部分原因是我想援引坎宁安定律。得出正确答案的最好方法就是发布错误的答案！
你对数据直觉的定义有共鸣吗？我错过了什么重要的东西吗？让我知道！我的电子邮件在这一页的底部。
我将在接下来的几个月里建立一些自助式培训，帮助Mozilla的非数据人员建立数据直觉。我宁愿现在就错，而不是明年！
请随时通过电子邮件分享任何反馈！你可以在gmail上通过harterrt联系到我。期待您的回音！
note_en:
Last week, one of my peers asked me to explain what I meant by "Data Intuition",and I realized I really didn't have a good definition.That's a problem! I refer to data intuition all the time!
 Data intuition is one of the three skills I interview new data scientists for(along with statistics and technical skills).In fact, I just spent the first nine months of 2020building Mozilla's data intuition.I'm really surprised to realize I can't point toa good explanation of what I'm trying to cultivate.
   In other words, it's harder to mislead someone with dataif they have strong data intuition.Think of this as  a defense against the dark data arts.
   Someone with strong data intuition can quickly spot "data-stink"(a close cousin to " code smell").These are data issues that don't necessarily invalidate an analysis,but certainly draw suspicion on the results.For example:
 An analysis prominently reports a seemingly  arbitrary metric - 4-day retention increased by 0.5%! Where did 4-day retention come from? Don't we usually track 7-day retention? This needs more attention before I trust the results.
 An analysis reports  extraordinary results where nominal results are expected - this feature increased retention by 10%! But, past efforts were trying to increase retention by 0.5% - and isn't retention already 90%? How'd we get and increase of 10%?
 These are extreme examples. Usually the problems are more subtleand result in a general sense of uneasiness with the results(that's why it's called "intuition").
 It's clear to me that data intuition is  related to product intuition,though these  are different skills.Product intuition can contextualize our resultsand make it easier to identify extraordinary claims in analyses.To know a 10% gain in retention is ridiculouswe need to know that users retain pretty well already.
  Strong data intuition can also help you spot issues with how the analysis was designed.Things like: how did the author collect data? Is it a representative sample?Do they need to have an experiment to establish causation?
 Here's an example -say an analysis reports that Firefox users who create a Firefox accountretain 10% higher than users who don't.By default, a lot of folks interpret this to mean thatif we invest some time in helping users open accountswe'll see an increase in retention.Folks with stronger data intuition will instead recognize these results are just correlational (not causational).
 Users who use the product a lot tend to stick around longer.Users who open an account are more active users, thus they retain better.Users who  crash Firefox are more active users, and also retain  better.
 I think this intuition is more than just understanding statistics well.A strong stats background can help me identify issueswhen reading the  methods section of a white paper.Strong data intuition helps me determine how much I trustresults I hear about in a  news headline.Data intuition helps me establish whether results are true-enough.
  I almost defined data intuition as a type of skepticism,but I think this is a bad characterization.Skepticism over-focuses on disregarding results.
 Intuition is more than being skeptical.It's  incorporating new data as part of a body of existing knowledge.A lot of times, that means deciding new incoming data are inconsistentand need more investigating before we can trust them.But other times, it means changing our opinions in the face of new datathat are more authoritative than our existing body of knowledge.
  I want to hear your thoughts on this.I'm posting this definition publicly in part because I want to invoke Cunningham's Law.The best way to get to the right answer is to post the wrong answer!
 Does this definition for data intuition resonate with you?Am I missing something important? Let me know! My email is at the bottom of this page.
 I'm spending the next few month building some self-service trainingsto help non-data people at Mozilla build data intuition.I'd rather be wrong now than next year!
      Feel free to share any feedback via email! You can reach me at  harterrt on gmail. Look forward to hearing from you!
title: 定义数据直观

尼斯市长称这是一起恐怖袭击，并将其归因于伊斯兰激进主义。[3][4]根据市长的说法，袭击者在被捕后继续高喊真主至大。[2]。
近年来，法国发生了几起圣战恐怖袭击事件，既有伊斯兰国和基地组织恐怖组织实施的袭击，也有受这两个组织启发的独狼恐怖分子发动的袭击。尼斯是2016年卡车袭击的现场，那次袭击导致86人死亡；袭击发生在距离2020年袭击现场约0.8公里(0.50英里)的地方。几天后，牧师雅克·哈默尔(Jacques Hamel)在诺曼底教堂遇袭身亡。[3][5]。
事件发生前两周，历史教师塞缪尔·帕蒂在向学生展示描绘伊斯兰先知穆罕默德的“查理周刊”漫画后，被一名车臣穆斯林谋杀并斩首。[6]事件发生后，土耳其总统雷杰普·塔伊普·埃尔多安(Recep Tayyip ErdoğAn)呼吁抵制法国产品。随之而来的是穆斯林世界各地的几场抗议活动，法国总统伊曼纽尔·马克龙的照片被焚烧，高呼反对法国的口号。[7]。
据尼斯市长克里斯蒂安·埃斯特罗西称，袭击发生在尼斯市中心Jean Médecin大道上的罗马天主教大教堂尼斯圣母院内或周围。据报道，袭击者反复高喊“真主至大”，甚至在他被回应的警察开枪并拘留之后也是如此。据警方消息人士称，两名女子和一名男子被杀，其中一名女子被斩首。埃斯特罗西说，其中两名受害者在教堂内死亡，第三名受重伤逃离教堂后在附近的酒吧死亡；其中一名受害者是教堂典狱长。[1][2][8]。
内政部长杰拉尔德·达尔马宁(Gerald Darmanin)表示，警方正在该市采取行动。[2]一支拆弹小分队赶到犯罪现场，全副武装的反恐警察开始在大教堂周围的街道上巡逻。[9]尼斯袭击事件发生后的几个小时内，一名男子在阿维尼翁附近被警方开枪打死，他用手枪威胁路人，并高喊：真主至大！此外，同一天，一名保安在法国驻沙特阿拉伯领事馆的持刀袭击中受伤。[1]。
三人在袭击中丧生：他们被确认为一名70多岁的女子，她被斩首；一名45岁的男子担任教堂典狱长；一名30多岁的女子因重伤逃离教堂后在附近的酒吧死亡。[10]。
刺杀消息传来后，国民议会举行了默哀仪式。[3]政府证实，总统伊曼纽尔·马克龙将在上午晚些时候视察袭击现场。[11]
欧洲议会议长大卫·萨索利、西班牙首相佩德罗·桑切斯和意大利总理朱塞佩·孔戴都发表声明，向法国人民表示哀悼。[12]事件发生几个小时后，马来西亚前总理马哈蒂尔·穆罕默德(Mahathir Mohamad)表示，穆斯林“有权惩罚”法国人。[13]土耳其外交部发表声明，向法国人民表示哀悼，并表示“任何理由都不能使杀人或暴力合法化或开脱。那些在神圣的礼拜场所实施这场野蛮袭击的人显然没有任何宗教、人道或道德价值观。

Jump to navigation  Jump to search  On 29 October 2020, three people were killed in a stabbing attack at  Notre-Dame de Nice, a  Roman Catholic  basilica, in  Nice, France. One of the victims, a woman, was beheaded by the attacker.  [1] Several additional victims were injured. The attacker, who was shot by the police, was taken into custody.  [2]
 The Mayor of Nice said this was a "terrorist attack", attributing it to  Islamic radicalism.  [3]  [4] According to the mayor, the attacker continued shouting " Allahu Akbar" after he was arrested.  [2]
   In recent years, France has seen several  jihadist  terrorist attacks, carried out by both  Islamic State and  al-Qaeda terrorist cells, and by  lone wolf terrorists inspired by the two groups. Nice was the scene of  a truck attack in 2016, which resulted in the deaths of 86 people; the attack occurred about 0.8 kilometres (0.50 mi) away from the scene of the 2020 attack. Days after that, a priest,  Jacques Hamel, was  murdered in an attack on a church at  Normandy.  [3]  [5]
 Two weeks prior this incident, Samuel Paty, a history teacher, was  murdered and beheaded by a Chechen Muslim after showing to his students   Charlie Hebdo  cartoons depicting the Islamic prophet  Muhammad.  [6] Following the events, Turkish President  Recep Tayyip Erdoğan called for a boycott of French products. Several protests across the  Muslim world followed, in which photos of French President  Emmanuel Macron were burned, chanting against France.  [7]
  According to Nice Mayor  Christian Estrosi, the attack occurred in or around the premises of  Notre-Dame de Nice, a  Roman Catholic  basilica, situated on the  Avenue Jean Médecin in the centre of  Nice. The attacker reportedly shouted "Allahu Akbar!" repeatedly, even after he was shot and detained by responding police. According to police sources, two women and one man were killed, with one of the women being decapitated. Estrosi said that two of the victims died inside the church, while the third died at a nearby bar after escaping from the church with serious injuries; one of the victims was the church warden.  [1]  [2]  [8]
 Interior Minister  Gerald Darmanin said a police operation was underway in the city.  [2] A bomb disposal unit responded to the crime scene, while heavily armed anti-terror police officers began patrolling the streets around the cathedral.  [9] Within hours of the Nice attack, a man was shot and killed by police near  Avignon after he threatened passersby with a handgun and shouted, "Allahu Akbar!" In addition, a security guard was injured in a knife attack at the French consulate in  Saudi Arabia that same day.  [1]
  Three people were killed in the attack: they were identified as a woman in her 70s, who was beheaded; a 45-year-old man who served as the church warden; and a woman in her 30s who died at a nearby bar after escaping from the church with serious injuries.  [10]
   A moment of silence was held at the  National Assembly after news of the stabbing arrived.  [3] The government confirmed that President  Emmanuel Macron would visit the site of the attack later in the morning.  [11]
  European Parliament President  David Sassoli,  Spanish Prime Minister  Pedro Sánchez, and Italian Prime Minister  Giuseppe Conte all released statements expressing their condolences to the French people.  [12] Just hours after the incident occurred, former  Prime Minister of Malaysia  Mahathir Mohamad said that Muslims “have the right to punish” the French.  [13]  Turkish foreign ministry released a statement expressing their condolences to the French people and stating that “no reason could legitimise or excuse killing someone or violence. Those who conducted this savage attack at a sacred place of worship do not clearly share any religious, humane or moral values."  [14]

QAnon的一个黑暗讽刺一直是，认为特朗普总统正在向精英自由派恋童癖者组成的阴谋发动战争的阴谋论，在用户交换儿童色情作品的图像板8chan上声名鹊起。
但这种讽刺可能还有更黑暗、更深层的原因：琼斯母亲发现，8chan及其后续网站8kun的所有者吉姆·沃特金斯(Jim Watkins)控制着一家公司，该公司托管了数十个域名，这些域名表明这些域名与儿童色情有关。
虽然琼斯妈妈没有访问这些域名，是因为与查看儿童性虐待材料相关的严格法律，但互联网注册和托管数据至少表明，沃特金斯从名字明确与恋童癖有关的域名中获利-这正是QAnon的追随者表示他们有动机要结束的事情。这些域名包括“青春期前”、“女学生”和“儿童”等术语，以及代表生殖器的图形术语和“强奸”、“爱情”等词语。目前还不清楚这些域名目前提供的服务是什么(如果有的话)。然而，一项对几年前Archive.org从其中一个收集的元数据的分析显示，数十个文件名和链接包含高度提示性的术语，包括“xxxpreten”、“Children”，以及对12至15岁女孩的性引用。
根据网络安全公司Farsight Security汇编的记录，其中一些域名可以追溯到20世纪90年代末，可能不再活跃，而另一些域名目前解析为沃特金斯的公司N.T.Technology控制的IP地址。Farsight Security是一家网络安全公司，将详细说明域名、IP地址、域名服务器和其他数字资产之间关系的历史路由数据存档。
一些专家认为，沃特金斯在维护8kun上的QAnon账户方面发挥了积极作用。他驳斥了琼斯母亲关于这些域名的问题，称其为“试图抹黑[他]的名字，并打印一些可怕的东西”，并对这些域名为什么会出现在N.T.Technology的服务器上做出了不同的解释。“我们不是儿童色情制作人，我们也不主持儿童色情，我们不会容忍这一点，”他坚持说。
一群反Q的研究人员，包括激进黑客组织“匿名者”的创始人之一奥布里·科特尔，将沃特金斯与域名的联系引起了琼斯母亲的注意。另一名研究人员因避免受到沃特金斯支持者的骚扰而要求不具名，他应琼斯母亲的要求审查了历史域名和路由信息，并同意记录显示，N.T.技术“在很长一段时间内托管了许多以CP(儿童色情)为主题的域名。”
通过结合使用开源数据和商业数据，研究人员证实，域名指向注册到N.T.Technology的IP地址，并且在显式域名处于活动状态时，IP地址被路由到该公司的网络。
其中一个域名创建于1998年，由N.T.Technology托管，该公司标榜自己是“互联网世界的巨头，支持地球上一些最大的网站”，将沃特金斯列为其管理员，并指定他拥有的另一家控股公司Is It Wet Yet Inc.作为管理机构。根据历史域名和网络档案记录，多年来，该域名已经与数十个子域名联系在一起，这些子域名是主域名的更具体、更集中的分支，它们的名称结合了“青春期前”、“绑架”和“强奸”等术语。当被问及具体的域名时，沃特金斯说“我真的不知道”。
内华达州的记录显示，N.T.Technology成立于1998年。就在上周，在信息安全记者布莱恩·克雷布斯(Brian Krebs)报道该公司的公司注册已经失效后，该州的记录进行了更新，将沃特金斯列为该公司的总裁和董事。
弗雷德里克·布伦南(Fredrick Brennan)创立了8chan，但在2016年将图像板的控制权移交给了沃特金斯。后来，他们在网站的方向上出现了公开的分歧。布伦南告诉琼斯母亲，如果“鉴于吉姆在8chan上对这个问题的态度，他不会感到惊讶”，他解释说，当布伦南提议部署机器学习工具来阻止董事会上的儿童性虐待材料时，沃特金斯“总是会找到更重要的事情要做”。沃特金斯驳斥了布伦南的说法，称“我们使用机器学习所做的事情是私人的。”
虽然记录8chan臭名昭著的有毒环境的媒体报道指出，那里继续发现儿童色情制品，但在一次电话中，沃特金斯说，8chan在收购该网站时，在儿童性虐待内容方面有很多问题，但他声称，在他的领导下，该网站被删除，因为它成为了互联网上最严格的网站之一。
沃特金斯说，除了拥有托管这些域名的公司之外，他与任何一个域名都没有直接联系，尽管他的公司的电子邮件和他的名字出现在其中几个域名的注册中。“大概有3万或4万个域名与我的名字相关联。他们中很少有我与之有任何关系的，“他说。沃特金斯推测，如果他参与分发儿童色情作品，他“很久以前就会被逮捕”。
当被问及批次中某个域名的名称表明该域名托管了儿童性虐待材料时，沃特金斯声称，当他注意到这个域名时，他已经在“15到20年前”删除了这个域名。他说，这些域名的名称让人联想到儿童色情，在他的公司托管时没有任何内容，而是为了域名抢注而建立的，这种做法是，一旦域名所有者发现愿意购买引用儿童内衣和性虐待未成年人等内容的URL，就会获利。在随后的谈话中，他暗示这些子域是作为世纪之交常见的搜索引擎优化策略的一部分创建的。
沃特金斯声称不知道其元数据显示其托管了与儿童性虐待相关的文件名和链接的域名，并表示，如果该域名真的托管了此类材料，该域名本身就会被删除。“如果出现问题，我们会移除、删除并报告，”他说。沃特金斯说，除非引起他的注意，否则他不会审查客户，也不会采取行动来确定他们是否利用他的服务进行非法行为：“这几乎是我能想象到的最不美国化的事情，先生。这就是苏联人会做的事，或者共产主义的中国人会做的事。我们不再有苏联人了。我们打败了他们。“。
现年56岁的沃特金斯与色情有着悠久的联系，这可以追溯到他在90年代中期创建的一个网站，当时该行业正在向在线转型。根据2016年斯普林特的一篇报道，到2001年，沃特金斯作为美国陆军直升机修理工的工作把他带到了菲律宾，在那里他从色情网站扩展到网络服务，托管针对逃避日本淫秽法律的消费者的成人网站。他说，他选择了N.T.Technology这个无伤大雅的名字，是为了掩盖他的客户妻子可能会质疑的信用卡费用。
QAnon阴谋论始于4chan，一个沃特金斯没有参与运营的网站，最终在2017年迁移到8chan。8chan最终在2019年8月被关闭，当时它的域名托管和网络安全提供商在几名极右翼枪手使用该网站传播他们的宣言后放弃了它。沃特金斯为Q提供了一个继续发帖的地方，重新启动并将图像板重新命名为8Kun，誓言更严格地控制儿童色情和其他非法内容。
Qanon的无序扩张和不断进化可能会让它很难追随。这件事始于4chan上的一张海报，他声称自己是联邦政府的一名成员，拥有高级别的“Q”安全许可。来自Q的帖子掉下了神秘的面包屑，据称这些面包屑揭示了一个由自由派精英运营的恋童癖团伙。这一阴谋认为唐纳德·特朗普不顾所谓的“深层国家”的反对，正在秘密打击这个阴谋集团，并在总统的支持者中找到了热情的支持者。
沃特金斯在QAnon中的确切角色是模糊的。至少，人们普遍认为，他在为这场运动提供了一个蓬勃发展的平台方面发挥了重要作用，因为他公开鼓励了这一阴谋。当他被传唤到国会作证时，他戴着一个“Q”别针。就在几周前，他在凤凰城的QAnon会议上做了专题演讲。事实核查网站Logically.ai本月报道，除了在8kun上运营Qanon的家之外，Watkins似乎还在促进Qanon在其他地方的在线社区中心方面发挥了作用。
8chan的最初创始人布伦南暗示沃特金斯参与的程度更深，他假设沃特金斯利用自己作为8kun所有者的权力，实质上劫持了自称是Q的账户。其他研究人员和记者提出了沃特金斯参与其中或了解谁在运营该账户的可能性。沃特金斯否认了这一说法，或曾担任Q。
尽管沃特金斯与QAnon账户没有直接关系，但作为Q的积极支持者和该运动的东道主，沃特金斯是一个阴谋的不可分割的支持者，这个阴谋错误地将政治对手和无辜的旁观者贴上恋童癖的标签-尽管他自己显然在从吸引真实事物的言辞中获利。

note_en:
For indispensable reporting on the coronavirus crisis, the election, and more, subscribe to the   Mother Jones Daily newsletter.
 One dark irony of QAnon has always been that the conspiracy theory, which holds that President Trump is waging a war on a cabal of elite liberal pedophiles, rose to prominence on 8chan, an imageboard where users  swapped child pornography.
 But that irony may have a darker, deeper layer:  Mother Jones has uncovered that Jim Watkins, the owner of 8chan and its successor site, 8kun, controls a company that hosted scores of domains whose names suggest they are connected to child pornography.
  While  Mother Jones did not visit the domains because of strict laws related to viewing child sex abuse material, internet registration and hosting data suggest at a minimum that Watkins profited from domains with names explicitly related to pedophilia—the very thing that QAnon followers say that they’re motivated to end. The domains’ names include terms such as “preteen,” “schoolgirl,” and “child” alongside graphic terms for genitalia and words like “rape” and “love.”  It’s unclear what, if anything, is currently being served at the domains.  However, an analysis of metadata collected years ago from one by archive.org shows dozens of filenames and links containing highly suggestive terms, including “xxxpreteen,” “children,” and sexual references to girls aged 12 to 15.
 Some of the domains date back to the late 1990s and may no longer be active, while others currently resolve to IP addresses controlled by Watkins’ company, N.T. Technology, according to records compiled by Farsight Security, a cybersecurity company that archives historical routing data detailing relationships among domain names, IP addresses, name servers, and other digital assets.
 Watkins, who some experts   believe has an active role in maintaining the QAnon account on 8kun, dismissed  Mother Jones‘ questions about the domains as “an attempt to smear [his] name and print something awful,” offering varying explanations why they were on N.T. Technology’s servers.  “We’re not child pornographers, and we don’t host child pornography, and we don’t condone that,” he insisted.
 A group of anti-Q researchers that includes Aubrey Cottle, a founder of the activist hacker collective Anonymous, brought Watkins’ connection to the domains to  Mother Jones’ attention. Another researcher,  who asked not to be named to avoid harassment from Watkins supporters,  reviewed the historical domain and routing information at  Mother Jones’ request and agreed the records showed that N.T. Technology “hosted many domains with CP [child pornography] themed names over an extended period of time.”
 Using a combination of open-source and commercially available data, the researcher confirmed that the domains pointed to IP addresses registered to N.T. Technology, and that the IP addresses were routed to the company’s  network while the explicit domains were active.
 One domain created in 1998 and hosted by  N.T. Technology, which  bills itself as “a powerhouse in the internet world, [s]upporting some of the largest sites on Earth,” lists Watkins as its administrator and names a separate holding company he owns, Is It Wet Yet Inc., as the administration organization. According to historical domain and web archive records, the domain has, over the years, been associated with dozens of subdomains—more specific and focused offshoots of the main domain—whose names combine terms like “preteen,” “kidnap,” and “rape.” They include a comprehensive system of subdomains referencing age ranges as young as 10. When asked about the specific domain, Watkins said “I have really no idea about” it.
 Nevada records show N.T. Technology was formed in 1998. Just last week, following a   story from information security journalist Brian Krebs noting the company’s corporate registration had lapsed, the state’s records were updated, listing Watkins as both the company’s president and director.
 Fredrick Brennan founded 8chan, but turned over control of the imageboard to Watkins in 2016. They later had a public split over the site’s direction. Brennan told  Mother Jones that he would not be surprised if “Jim was involved in pedophile sites going back that far given his attitude towards the problem on 8chan,” explaining that Watkins “would always find something more important to do” when Brennan proposed deploying machine learning tools to block child sexual abuse material from the board. Watkins dismissed Brennan’s claims, saying that “what we do with machine learning is private.”
 Though press reports documenting 8chan’s  notorious and  toxic  environment noted that child pornography  continued to be found there, i n a phone call, Watkins said that 8chan “had a lot of problems” with child sexual abuse content when he acquired the site, but claimed it was removed under him as “i t became one of the strictest websites on the internet.”
  Beyond owning the company that hosted them, Watkins said he has no direct link to any of the domains, despite his companies’ email and his name showing up on registrations for several of them.  “ There’s probably 30 or 40,000 domain names associated with my name. Very few of them I have had anything to do with,” he said. Watkins speculated that he “would have been arrested long ago,” if he were involved in distributing child porn.
 When asked about a particular domain in the batch with a name suggesting it hosted child sex abuse material, Watkins claimed he had deleted it “15 to 20 years ago” when it came to his attention. He said the domains with names suggestive of child pornography had no content when hosted by his company, but had been established for domain squatting, a practice where the domains’ owners would profit once they found willing buyers for URLs referencing things like children’s underwear and the sexual abuse of minors. In a subsequent conversation, he suggested the subdomains were created as part of a common turn-of- the-century search engine optimization tactic.
 Watkins claimed not to know the domain whose metadata showed it hosting child sex abuse-related filenames and links, and said that if the domain actually hosted such material the domain itself would have been removed. “If there was ever a problem we removed and deleted and reported,” he said. Watkins said that he did not vet clients nor take action to determine whether they used his services to commit illegal acts unless it was brought to his attention: “It’s about the most un-American thing I can imagine, sir. That’s what the Soviets would be doing, or the communist Chinese. And we don’t have Soviets anymore. We defeated them.”
 The now  56-year-old  Watkins has a long connection to pornography stretching back to a site he founded in the mid 90s as the industry was transitioning online. By 2001, Watkins’s work as a US Army helicopter repairman   took him to the Philippines, where he expanded from porn sites into web services, hosting adult sites targeting consumers evading Japanese obscenity laws, according to a   2016  Splinter story. He has   said he chose N.T. Technology’s innocuous name to provide cover for credit card charges that might be questioned by his customers’ wives.
 The QAnon conspiracy theory began on 4chan, a site Watkins had no role in running, eventually migrating to 8chan in 2017. 8chan was ultimately shut down in August of 2019 when its domain hosting and cybersecurity providers dropped it after several far-right wing shooters used the site to disseminate their manifestos. Watkins provided Q a place to continue posting by restarting and rebranding the imageboard as 8Kun,   vowing to moderate child porn and other illegal content more strictly.
 QAnon’s sprawl and constant evolution can make it difficult to follow. It started with a poster on 4chan who claimed to be a member of the federal government with high-level “Q” security clearance. Posts from Q dropped cryptic breadcrumbs that purportedly shed light on a pedophile ring being run by liberal elites. The conspiracy, which holds that Donald Trump is secretly battling this cabal despite opposition from the so-called “deep state,” has found ardent supporters among the president’s base.
  Watkins’ precise role in QAnon is murky. At a minimum, he is   widely recognized as   having played an instrumental part in giving the movement a platform where it could flourish as he has publicly encouraged the conspiracy. When   he was subpoenaed to testify before Congress, he wore a   “Q” pin. Just weeks ago, he   was a featured speaker at a QAnon conference in Phoenix. In addition to running QAnon’s home on 8kun, the fact-checking site Logically.ai   reported this month that Watkins appears to be playing a role in facilitating online QAnon community hubs elsewhere.
 Brennan, 8chan’s original founder, has suggested Watkins’ involvement goes deeper, positing he took advantage of his power as the owner of 8kun to essentially hijack the account that claims to be Q. Other   researchers and   journalists have raised the possibility that Watkins has some involvement or knowledge of who runs the account. Watkins has denied that claim, or having acted as Q.
 Even if he has no direct relationship to the QAnon account, as an active supporter of Q and the movement’s host, Watkins is an inextricable sustainer of a conspiracy that has falsely branded political opponents and innocent bystanders as pedophiles—despite his own apparent role in profiting from words that appeal to the real thing.

title: QAnon应该完全是为了保护孩子们。其主要启用程序似乎有托管子色情域名。
title_en: QAnon is Supposed to Be All About Protecting Kids. Its Primary Enabler Appears to Have Hosted Child Porn Domains.

这是第一期的SRE团队。在这里，我将与大家分享一些有趣的公司是如何实施现场可靠性工程实践的。什么是有效的，他们面临着什么挑战，以及对也在建立SRE团队的公司有什么建议。
接下来的3期已经准备好，将在未来几天发布。请确保您订阅了直接在您的收件箱中接收它们。
我有机会和约翰谈谈。在哈什，他领先于SRE。我们讨论了他们是如何构建SRE组织的，以及他们对新团队有什么建议。
哈希是巴西人金融科技打造的下一代支付基础设施。他们目前提供终端解决方案，商家可以接受实体卡支付。它就像广场，但是在巴西。他们还有更多的事情要做。
Hash针对他们正在运行的业务类型使用非常高级的堆栈。大多数金融科技在技术方面都是保守的，但哈希不是这样。他们用Istio在库伯内斯经营集装箱。使用普罗米修斯、警报管理器和Grafana进行监控和警报。以及用于CI和CD的GitLab。
Hash的团队正在使用Google SRE原则。SRE团队在公司拥有自主权。他们制定了成熟度策略，并运行任务关键型应用程序。但前提是他们必须通过准备情况检查表。SRE团队提供了产品团队可以使用的标准工具。SRE拥有CI/CD管道、基础架构代码等。但开发人员可以自由贡献功能或编写自己的定制版本。自定义功能将失去SRE团队支持。
选择退出或没有为SRE准备好应用程序的产品团队自己运行。他们拥有对所需资源的完全访问权限。在这些情况下，与SRE团队的接口最少。负责该产品的团队在K8中有自己的命名空间。受网络策略、资源配额和其他因素的限制。它们可以快速迭代，而不会危及集群中的其他产品容器。
从基础设施资源到监控警报，所有东西都是由Git代码管理的。这使得任何更改(包括基础架构和警报)都能以极快的速度进行。他们还对基础设施资源运行测试。它确保新的更改不会破坏现有的行为。
SRE团队负责他们运行的产品的SLO和SLA。定义明确的警报将与产品相关的问题发送给产品团队。寻呼仅在与他们负责的事情相关的关键事件中发生。
Hash团队拥有健康的DevOps文化。产品团队为基础架构代码做出贡献，并对平台有很好的理解。这使得Hash的SRE团队只需2个人即可完成所有工作！运行平台应用程序、跟踪指示器、创建进程等等。他们是一个由22名产品工程师组成的两人团队。这是1：11的SRE与产品工程师比率。它们正在快速增长，预计到今年年底将达到40名工程学人员。
业务的快速增长产生了一些副作用。它需要改善与产品团队的沟通，以便对平台服务进行重大更改。而制作这些过程是一项具有挑战性的任务。成熟度模型规范正在发生很大变化。接受公司其他部门的捐款。随着团队的快速发展，保持这种文化也是具有挑战性的。
使Hash能够快速、高质量扩展的关键因素之一是文化。该团队的一个重要教训是，在招聘时不要在文化契合度上妥协。吸引新人来减轻两人团队的压力是很有诱惑力的。但是一个不合适的人会破坏多年的工作。
SRE团队@Hash正在招聘！如果你想加入这个伟大的团队和项目，请联系约翰。
然后和你工作中的朋友分享这个词。这将确保他们获得其他SRE团队正在发生的最新情况。
note_en:
This is the first issue of SRE Teams. Here I share how interesting companies are implementing Site Reliability Engineering practices. What is working, what challenges they face, and any advice for companies also building SRE Teams.
 The next 3 issues are ready and will be released in the coming days. Make sure you subscribe to receive them directly in your inbox.
   I got the chance to speak with Jhonn. He is ahead of SRE at Hash. We talked about how they are structuring their SRE organization and any advice they have for new teams.
    Hash is a Brazilian fintech building the next-generation of payments infrastructure. They currently provide terminal solutions where merchants can accept physical card payments. It’s like Square but in Brazil. And they have a lot more coming up.
  Hash uses a very advanced stack for the type of business they are running. Most Fintech are conservative on the tech side, but this is not the case for Hash. They run containers on Kubernetes with Istio. Use Prometheus, Alert Manager, and Grafana for monitoring and alerts. And Gitlab for CI and CD.
  The team at Hash is using the Google SRE principles. The SRE team has autonomy in the company. They have maturity policies in place and operate mission-critical applications. But only if they pass the readiness checklist. The SRE team provides standard tooling that product teams can use. SRE owns CI/CD pipelines, infrastructure code, and others. But developers are free to contribute to features or write their own customized versions. Custom features lose the SRE team support.
  Product teams that opt-out or don’t have their application ready for SRE run on their own. They have full access to the resources they need. The interface with the SRE team is minimal in these cases. The team in charge of the product gets its own namespace in k8s. Limited by network policies, resource quotas, and others. They can iterate fast without risking other product containers in the cluster.
  Everything is code managed by Git, from infrastructure resources to monitoring alerts. This allows for an extreme speed for any change, including infrastructure and alerts. They also run tests on infrastructure resources. It makes sure new changes don’t break existing behaviors.
  The SRE team is in charge of SLOs and SLAs of the products they run. Well defined alerts route product-related issues to product teams. Paging happens only in critical events related to something they are in charge of.
  The Hash team has a healthy DevOps culture. Product teams contribute to infrastructure code and have a good understanding of the platform. This allowed the SRE team at Hash to do everything with only 2 people! Running the platform applications, tracking indicators, creating processes, and many more. They are a 2 person team in a 22 product engineers company. This is a 1:11 SRE to product engineer ratio. They are growing fast and are expected to reach 40 people in engineering by year-end.
  The rapid growth of the business created some side-effects. It requires improved communication with product teams for significant changes to platform services. And making these processes is a challenging task. The maturity model spec is evolving a lot. Receiving contributions from other areas of the company. Maintaining this culture is also challenging with the rapid growth of the teams.
  One of the critical things allowing Hash to scale fast with quality is culture. One of the key lessons from the team is not to compromise on culture-fit when hiring. It’s tempting to bring new people to lower the pressure of 2 people team. But someone with the wrong fit would break the work of years.
 The SRE Team @ Hash is hiring! Reach out to  Jhonn if you want to join this great team and project.
    Then share the word with your friends at work. This will make sure they get updates on what is happening with other SRE Teams.
title: 现实世界的SRE：FAANG公司正在做的不是什么
title_en: Real-world SRE: What not FAANG companies are doing

note:
如今，加拿大房地产领域的洗钱活动是一个被广泛接受的现实，但其影响却并非如此。政府和学者仍在争论需要多少资金才能扭曲一个市场。事实是，扭曲任何资产市场并不需要很多东西。这是股票市场自20世纪20年代以来一直在处理的问题，也是它受到如此严格监管的原因。
了解洗钱如何影响价格的关键是了解边际买家。如果你了解价格是如何设定的，用不了多久就会发现问题不在于钱的多少。价格扭曲可能是资本流动的结果，也可能是边际买家意图的结果。
首先，我们来澄清一下洗钱问题。洗钱是使非法收益看起来合法的过程。这些收益可能来自可怕的活动，比如芬太尼的贩运。有时，它不那么邪恶，比如赚取的收入可以逃避一个国家专断的资本管制。然而，所有这些都是非法的，人们都在试图隐藏它。有几种方法可以做到这一点-但所有方法都遵循相同的基本过程。
洗钱通常分三个阶段进行-放置、分层和整合。配售是将现金引入合法系统。分层是通过多个账户进行多个交易，以混淆线索。整合是将资金重新投入到合法的系统中。正确的洗钱应该很难从合法的业务中区分出来。
最后一次，我们的目标是干净的钱。将现金长期存放在资产上并不典型--这些人不是投资者。也就是说，分层过程通常涉及到非常迅速地转移现金。快速流动的现金通常会留下后遗症，特别是如果它是通过房地产流动的话。要理解原因，你需要理解几个概念-边缘买家、洗钱和销售补偿。
边际买家是任何资产市场的重要组成部分，尤其是快速变化的资产市场。这是愿意为一项资产支付最高价格的人或公司。他们只占潜在买家池的一小部分，但实际上是购买资产的人。边际买家之间的竞争是资产价格升级的关键。每个市场都有一个正在上涨的市场，但技能和动机决定了结果的健康程度。
如果边际买家是理性投资者，他们会考虑流动性。他们的出价受到限制，因为他们需要能够盈利。理性的考虑有助于保持市场的理智。如果买家不受理性或逻辑的约束，事情就会开始变得草率。
一家年收入2000万美元的大麻公司的估值接近通用汽车？一套产生负现金流的投资公寓？这些东西的购买者并没有做出理性的决定。这并不意味着他们赚不到钱，但他们在玩一个更愚蠢的游戏。你希望下一个买家比你更不理性--不管你是否知道这是你的计划。当你有大量非理性的资金涌入时，很难弄清楚什么是真实的。
当你购买一项资产时，无论是一套房子还是一盎司粉红色库什，你都会努力让你的时间和金钱获得最佳的价值。你想要一笔交易。卖家试图从你那里榨取他们能得到的最高价格，而不是把你赶走。他们不想让你得到很高的价格。利益平衡来回运转，是一个正常运作的市场的基本组成部分。对立的利益有助于平衡事物，或多或少也会带来些许繁荣。
如果你在洗钱，情况就不是这样了。目标是以尽可能快的速度转移尽可能多的现金。这通常涉及大量资产，价格越高越好。特别是如果有经常性的付款部分的话。卖方和洗钱买方都想要可接受的最高价格。
当卖家找到这个买家时，他们往往觉得自己处于天才和彩票中奖者之间。利益之间的竞争是一致的，防止价格上涨的摩擦微乎其微。卖家认为他们精通的谈判技巧占上风。洗钱买家可以转移比他们要求的更多的钱。买家似乎是“非理性的”，但这只是市场现象。房地产中介毫无头绪，开始将这种行为合理化、常态化。没有更多的土地是一个流行的解释。
我们都知道价格是如何产生的。当房主找到他们喜欢的卖家时，他们会走进一间安静的密室，敲几下，然后砰的一声！多重列表服务吐出可比较的内容，也就是Ak.a。你们的指导员。Comps是一种花哨的方式，用来说明你周围的东西已经卖出去了，比如邻居的房子。然后，这些数字被用来建立一个基线价格，销售代理试图将其推高。
你家附近没有警察吗？没问题，我们可以用隔壁的街区。最终，分隔社区定价的任意线消失了。这时候你就会听到一些愚蠢的事情，比如“肖内西高地相邻”(Shaughnessy Heights毗邻)。这就像病毒一样从一个社区传播到下一个社区。
温哥华市房地产销售的一段时间。羊群行为可以在集群中观察到，因为人们支付的价格高于或低于标价-基于其他人是否在这样做。
更聪明的房地产经纪人已经发现了这里的问题。让我们来看一个例子，假设你要在公元前任何地方买房子。你可以看到，与去年相比，该社区的房屋平均涨幅为5%。你找到一个你准备好提供报价的地方，做一些研究，然后提出一个报价。突然，一个洗黑钱的人出现了，他向店主提供超过10%的折扣，要求“快点关门”。你不用太担心，你的经纪人告诉你，隔着几扇门的地方下周就要上市了。
不幸的是，新地方现在使用洗钱者拥有的房子作为补偿。现在的要价比你预期的高出10%，因为边际买家把价格定在了街上。其他人咬了它，在它“涨得太高”之前就把它买下了。现在，洗钱者的购买刚刚在系统中得到验证。但是等等，还有更多。
记住，洗钱的目的不是买房子，而是清洗钱财。他们又把房子挂牌了，比方说比买的高出10分。如果他们能把它变成一笔洗衣服的交易，并把它卖给另一个相关的洗衣商，就会获得加分。一个经常在街上购物的家庭会用你的洗衣机作为他们购物的补偿。通常只有在泡沫最严重的资产泡沫中才会出现的行为，可能会很快浮出水面。活跃的买家，无论是非法的还是合法的，都在竞争，推高了价格。
与定价基本面相关的加拿大房地产买家繁荣指数正在显现。一旦突破临界门槛，买家就不再使用基本资产。相反，他们求助于市场动力，而奖励的可能性就足以证明这一点。
现在，在这个例子中，仅仅几笔销售就可以帮助将COMPs推高21%。还将有数百笔交易验证两者之间的价格走势。每一次洗衣店注入资本，他们就会注入一个新的边际买家。一直以来，婴儿潮一代都在火上浇油，解释这是“赚取的权益”。如果你想要自己的，你需要像他们一样努力工作。在每个非理性的参与者进入市场时袖手旁观是一项令人精疲力竭的工作。婴儿潮一代还不得不为首付…存钱。两种方式，在雪地里还是别的什么地方。
还认为少量的钱不能影响物价吗？显然，你不熟悉另一个资产类别--股票。CNBC主持人吉姆·克雷默(Jim Cramer)曾咆哮说，他的基金可以用低至500万美元的资金操纵股价。NAV Singh Sarao伪造了价值1.7亿美元的订单，引发的事件导致DJIA在短短几分钟内损失了1万亿美元。注：订单是伪造的-这意味着他只有一小部分钱。更正式地说，学者们认为，通过瞄准流动性光谱的下半部分，交易员可以用不到50万美元的资金将股价提高1%。
问：不是你付了多少钱，而是你认为人们会付多少钱。
动态分层的一个例子，Nav Singh Sarao使用的欺骗技术。较低的圆点是发出的出价，只是有时作为交易执行。自由市场不能有效地确定参与者是否真诚地执行交易-这是自然价格平衡所必需的。
每种情况都不同，但都有两个共同点--影响力和意图。虽然没有那么多钱，但每个例子都引发了产生重大影响的事件。与其说实际交易重要，不如说是影响波动性。设定边际买家绝对算得上是影响市场走向的事件。
这些事件中的每一件都很容易被误认为是一起事故，这掩盖了意图。胖手指，疯狂的交易算法，和/或热切的市场买家。这些情况中的每一种都可能是由日常发生的常规事件造成的。现在看来，洗钱不太可能集中在系统性的房屋交易上，以抬高房价。然而，这可能是一种情况，无意中的市场不稳定只是一种副作用。
速度也可能在这里起着很大的作用。当现金进入一所房子时，它最终会被出售。这些现金可能会为了分层的目的通过多笔交易注入。这意味着用这笔钱和利润买了更多的房子。更复杂的操作还将分层与集成平台相结合。如果集成平台已在FINTRAC注册，则可获得加分。这样，整合平台还负责提交可疑交易报告。
再加上不透明的薪酬系统和封闭的数据，这真的很难捕捉到。购房者能够对房产购买进行自己尽职调查的可能性几乎为零。封闭的系统也意味着不会对交易进行大范围的分析。对于监管者以外的任何人来说，几乎没有办法真正能够确定这一点。
当加拿大的城市正在讨论黑钱是否会影响价格时，世界其他地方已经拿定了主意。透明国际英国公司发现，空壳公司和价格上涨之间存在显著的相关性。例如，伦敦有8.7万套住房属于匿名公司。根据Netzwerk Steuergerechtigkeit的Christoph Trautvetter的说法，伦敦黑钱的估计影响是价格上涨的20%。
英国伦敦房屋的平均售价。这一估计剔除了归因于洗钱影响的20%的年度收益。这一数字还假设在2008年之前没有进行过洗钱活动。LOL。
加拿大也在酝酿类似的情况，政客们只是不太愿意调查而已。加拿大透明国际发现，5万多套大多伦多住宅是由没有已知受益所有权的公司购买的。更糟糕的是，使用的200亿美元资金没有经过任何反洗钱检查。在温哥华，当地政客仍然声称洗钱被夸大了。与此同时，在欧洲议会，温哥华实际上被用作不透明所有权扭曲房价的例子。
通过房地产洗钱远不是什么新鲜事，但速度和数量都不是什么新鲜事。传统上，洗钱者会购买、持有、有时甚至出租这些地方。房地产交易缺乏审查，一直使其成为主要的着陆点。每个城市都有几个知名的家庭与当地的暴徒有联系，只是碰巧是在房地产行业。当成交量低而缓慢时，对房价的影响微乎其微。
将房地产视为全球大宗商品市场，使其速度快、成交量大。加拿大的房地产业鼓励外资。事实上，加拿大的银行公开帮助客户进行“安置”，模糊了存款记录。你放得越快，价格上涨就越快，他们就越欢迎外资--洗得越容易。
这一直是股市不得不应对的问题。股票发行，人为的交易量抬高了价格，洗钱者清算给了毫无戒心的受害者。股票市场提高了大型交易所的所有权透明度，使得这一点变得更加困难。然而，它仍然很常见，特别是在欧洲和亚洲的证券交易所。像对待股票市场一样对待房地产会鼓励同样类型的洗钱，而不是透明的。
有趣的事实：现已停业的温哥华证券交易所很受洗钱者的欢迎。当时非常受欢迎，福布斯在1989年称温哥华为“世界诈骗之都”。不列颠哥伦比亚省的协调执法单位早在1974年就警告政府交易所存在有组织犯罪。这些警告在很大程度上被忽视了。你是否也感觉到了一种模式？
洗钱不是价格高得多的唯一原因，但它助长了火上浇油。低利率和宽松的贷款让普通家庭能够提供流动性。如果洗钱者得不到干净的现金，他们就不会进行交易。如果没有贪婪的购房者出价过高，或者迅速转手，就没有吸引力。
然而，洗钱投资者可以影响市场的走向。房地产市场的好坏取决于边际买家设定的最后一个价格。如果那个边际买家在洗钱，他们就有多付钱的动机。普通家庭购买这种产品，提供薪酬验证和流动性。大多数家庭从来没有考虑过他们的流动性将从哪里来。
在你的收件箱里过得更好。或者，你知道的..。等着你们银行的经济学家下周重复这些见解吧。什么东西。
note_en:
Money laundering in Canadian real estate is a widely accepted fact of life these days, but the impact isn’t. Government and academics are still debating how much money is needed to distort a market. The truth is, not a whole lot is required to distort any asset market. This is a problem the stock market has been dealing with since the 1920s, and the reason it’s so highly regulated.
 The key to understanding how laundering impacts prices, is understanding the marginal buyer. If you understand how prices are set, it doesn’t take long to see it’s not the amount of money that’s the issue. Price distortions can be the result of capital velocity, and the intention of the marginal buyer.
  First, let’s clarify laundering. Money laundering is the process of making illegally-gained proceeds appear legit. Those proceeds can be from monstrous activity, like fentanyl trafficking. Sometimes it’s less nefarious, like earned income evading a country’s arbitrary capital controls. All of it is illegal however, and is people are trying to hide it. There’s a few ways to do it – but the all follow the same basic process.
 Money laundering is usually done in three phases – placement, layering, and integration. Placement is the introduction of cash into a legitimate system. Layering is conducting multiple transactions through multiple accounts, to obfuscate a trail. Integration is working the money back into the legit system. Properly laundered money should be extremely difficult to tell from legitimate business.
 One last time, the goal is clean money. Parking cash long term in assets is not typical – these aren’t investors. That said, the layering process usually involves moving cash around very quickly. Fast moving cash often leaves a wake, especially if it’s moving through real estate. To understand why, you need to understand a few concepts – marginal buyers, money laundering, and sales comps.
  The marginal buyer is an important part of any asset market, especially fast moving ones. This is the person(s) or company that’s willing to pay the most for an asset. They are a small percent of the potential buyer pool, but the ones that actually buy the assets. The competition between marginal buyers is key to asset price escalation. Every market has one on the way up, but skill and motive determine how healthy the outcome is.
 If the marginal buyer is a rational investor, they’re thinking about liquidity. They’re restrained in their bidding price, because they need to be able to make a profit. Rational consideration helps to keep a market sane. If the buyer isn’t bound by rationale or logic, things start to get sloppy.
 A cannabis company making $20 million a year in revenue fetching close to the valuation of GM? An investment condo that produces negative cash flow? The buyers of these things aren’t making rational decisions. It doesn’t mean they can’t make money, but they are playing a game of greater fool. You’re hoping that the next buyer is more irrational than you – whether you know that’s the plan or not. When you have an influx of irrational money, it’s hard to figure out what’s real.
  When you buy an asset, whether a home or an oz of pink kush, you try to get the best value for your time and money. You want a deal. The seller is trying to extract the maximum price they can get from you, without driving you away. They don’t want you to get a great deal. The balance of interests go back and forth, and is a fundamental part of a functioning market. Opposing interests help balance things, plus or minus a dash of exuberance.
 If you are money laundering, that’s not the case. The objective is to move as much cash, as fast as possible. This often involves large assets, and the bigger the price – the better. Especially if there’s a recurring payment component. Both the seller and the money laundering buyer want the highest acceptable price.
 Sellers often feel somewhere between a genius and a lottery winner when they find this buyer. Competition between interests align, and there’s minimal friction preventing prices from going higher. The seller assumes their master negotiation skills prevailed. The money laundering buyer gets to move more money than they were asking for. The buyer seems “irrational,” but that’s just the market. Real estate agents without a clue, begin to rationalize and normalize this behavior. There’s no more land is a popular explanation.
  We all know how prices are born. When a homeowner finds a selling agent they love, they go into a quiet backroom, make a few strokes, and boom! The multiple listing service spits out the comparables, a.k.a. your comps. Comps are a fancy way of saying what has sold around you, like the neighbor’s house. These numbers are then used to establish a baseline price, which a selling agent tries to push higher.
 No comps in your neighborhood? No problem, we’ll use the neighborhood next door. Eventually, the arbitrary line disappears that separates the pricing in neighborhoods. This is when you hear dumb things, like “Shaughnessy Heights adjacent.” This spreads like a virus, from one neighborhood to the next.
  A time-lapse of real estate sales in the City of Vancouver. Herd behavior can be observed in clusters, as people pay over or under the list price – based on whether other people are doing it.
    Smarter real estate agents can already spot the problem here. Let’s look at an example, say you’re shopping for a home in Anyplace, BC. You’re watching the homes in the neighborhood climb at an average of 5% from last year. You find a place you’re ready to put on offer on, do some research, and come up with an offer. All of a sudden, a money launderer shows up, and offers the owner 10% over ask for a “quick close.” You’re not too worried, your agent told you the place a few doors down is going to be on the market next week.
 Unfortunately, the new place now uses the home owned by the money launder as a comp. Now the ask is 10% more than you were expecting, because the marginal buyer set the price down the street. Someone else bites, and buys it before it “goes too high.” Now the money launderer’s buy was just validated in the system. But wait – there’s more.
 Remember, the goal of laundering isn’t to buy a house, it’s to clean the money. They list the home again, let’s say another 10 points higher than bought. Bonus points if they can turn it into a wash trade, and sell it to another associated launderer. A regular family shopping down the street uses your washing machine as a comp for their buy. Behavior typically only seen in the frothiest of asset bubbles, can surface quickly. Exuberant buyers, both illicit and legit, compete and drive prices higher.
  An index of exuberance Canadian real estate buyers are demonstrating, in relation to pricing fundamentals. Once above the critical threshold is breached, buyers are no longer using fundementals. Instead they resort to market momentum, and the possiblity of reward is justification enough.
   Now in this example, just a few sales would have helped to push the comps up to 21% higher. There would also be hundreds of sales validating the price movements in between. Each time the launder injects capital, they inject a new marginal buyer. The whole time, Boomers are stoking the coals on this fire, explaining this is “earned equity.” If you want your own, you need to work as hard as they did. Standing by as each irrational player enters the market is exhausting work. Boomers also had to save uphill for a down payment… both ways, in the snow or something.
  Still think a small amount of money can’t influence prices? Clearly you’re not familiar with another asset class – stocks. CNBC host Jim Cramer once ranted that his fund  could manipulate stock prices with as little as $5 million. Nav Singh Sarao, spoofing just $170 million worth of orders, set off events that led to the DJIA losing $1 trillion in just a few minutes. Note: the orders were spoofed – meaning he only had a fraction of the money. More formally, academics determined  traders can use less than $500,000 to raise a stock price 1%, by targeting the bottom half of the liquidity spectrum.
 Smack That Ask: It’s Not What You Pay, It’s What You Think People Will Pay
 An example of  Dynamic Layering , the spoofing technique used by Nav Singh Sarao. The lower dots are bids placed, that only sometimes execute as a trade. Free markets can’t effectively determine if participants are executing trades in good faith – required for natural price balance.
  Each of the situations are different, but have two common things – influence and intent. While not that much money, each example precipitated events that had a big impact. The actual trades weren’t so important, so much as influencing volatility. Setting the marginal buyer definitely counts as an event that influences market direction.
 Each one of these events are also easily mistaken for an accident, which conceals intent. Fat finger, trade algo gone wild, and/or eager market buyer. Each one of these situations could have been caused by regular, everyday occurrences. Now it’s unlikely that money laundering is focusing on systematic trading of homes to inflate prices. It could however, be one of the times an unintentional destabilization of a market is just a side effect.
 Velocity may also be playing a large roll here. When cash goes into one house, it’s eventually sold. That cash likely gets pumped through multiple transactions for the purposes of layering. That means more houses are being bought with the money, and profits. More sophisticated operations also have combine layering with an integration platform. Bonus points if the integration platform is registered with FINTRAC. That way the integration platform is also in charge of submitting suspicious transaction reports.
 Combine this with an opaque comp system with closed data, and it’s really hard to catch. The chances of buyers being able to do their own due diligence on a property buy is virtually nil. Closed systems also mean no wide scale analysis of the transaction. There’s very little way for anyone outside of regulators to actually be able to determine it.
  While Canadian cities are debating whether dirty money impacts prices, the rest of the world made up its mind. Transparency International UK found a significant correlation between shell companies, and elevated prices. London for instance, has 87,000 homes owned by anonymous companies. According to Christoph Trautvetter of Netzwerk Steuergerechtigkeit, the estimated impact from dirty money in London is 20% of the price increases.
  The average sale price of a London, UK home. The estimate removes the 20% of annual gains attributed to the influence of money laundering. The number also assumes no laundering was done prior to 2008. LOL.
   There’s a similar setup brewing in Canada, politicians are just a little less willing to look into it. Transparency International Canada  found 50,000+ Greater Toronto homes bought by companies without known beneficial ownership. Even worse, $20 billion of the funds used were not subject to any anti-money laundering checks. In Vancouver, local politicians are still claiming money laundering is over exaggerated. Meanwhile,  in European Parliament, Vancouver is literally being used as an example of opaque ownership distorting home prices.
  Laundering money through real estate is far from new, but the velocity and volume is. Traditionally, launderers would buy, hold, and sometimes even rent the places out. The  lack of scrutiny in real estate transactions, has always made it a prime landing spot. Every city has a few well known families connected to local mobs, that just happen to be in real estate. The impact to home prices are minimal when the volume is low and slow.
 Treating real estate like a global commodity market makes it fast and high volume. The real estate industry in Canada encourages foreign capital. In fact, Canadian banks openly helped clients with “placement,” obfuscating deposit trails. The faster you can place, the faster prices rise, and the more they welcome foreign capital – the easier the wash.
 This has always been an issue stock markets have had to deal with. Equity is issued, artificial volume inflates prices, and launderers liquidate to unsuspecting victims. Equity markets have increased ownership transparency on larger exchanges, making it more difficult. However, it’s still common, especially on  European and Asian stock exchanges. Treating real estate like a stock market encourages the same type of laundering, without the transparency.
 Fun fact: The now defunct Vancouver Stock Exchange was popular with money launderers. It was so popular,  Forbes called Vancouver the “Scam Capital of The World” in 1989. The Coordinated Law Enforcement Unit in British Columbia  warned the government of organized crime on the exchange as early as 1974. Those warnings were largely ignored. Are you also sensing a pattern here?
 Money laundering is not the sole reason for much higher prices, but it fans the flames. Low interest rates and easy lending allow regular families to provide liquidity. If a launderer can’t get clean cash, they don’t transact. There’s no appeal without house horny buyers overbidding comps, or rapidly flipping.
 Money laundering investors however, can influence the direction of the market. A real estate market is only as good as its last comp, set by the marginal buyer. If that marginal buyer was laundering money, they have motivation to overpay. Regular households buying into this, provide comp validation, and liquidity. Most households never consider where their liquidity is going to come from.
 Get Better Dwelling In Your Inbox. Or, you know... wait for your bank's economist to repeat the insights next week. Whateves.
title: 小小的洗钱可以对房地产价格产生重大影响(2019年)
title_en: A Little Money Laundering Can Have a Big Impact on Real Estate Prices (2019)

直到我在YouTube上看到一段名为“D&A；D遇见纽约”的视频，我才真正了解了“地下城与龙”。我和朋友玩过几次，但是我每次转弯都被绊倒了，我想我永远也学不会所有的规则。事实证明，“D&amp；D遇见纽约”是“维度20：不眠之城”(Dimension20：The Unsleing City)的第一集，这是一部重演的D&A；D秀。在前五分钟，观众被介绍给插头皮特，这是一位巫师，他在接受了顶级手术后不久就去见了他的医生。我被吸引住了。
我没有意识到D&amp；D可以是这样的--它不一定要存在于我见过和听说过的中世纪幻想世界中。尽管如此，闯入一项充斥着行话和规则并有把关历史的活动可能会让人望而生畏。“连线”采访了一些专家，讨论如何入门，以及身份在桌面角色扮演游戏中的作用。
最困难的部分，就像大多数事情一样，是开始。但你真的只需要把几个朋友聚在一起，边走边想，内容创作者兼研发人员B·戴夫·沃尔特斯(B.Dave Walters)说。沃尔特斯说：“不要对规则过于强调。”“不要对你应该做的事情过分强调。”取而代之的是，想想你最喜欢的奇幻电影、电视剧、书籍，甚至是你最喜欢的角色，然后从那里开始。
沃尔特斯说：“在南方长大的黑人小孩，没有多少机会感受到强大。”“这也是D&amp；D最初给我的东西之一--一个感受英雄气概的机会。不只是读关于超级英雄的书或看电影，而是真正走出去过超级英雄的生活。“。
沃尔特斯从13岁起就开始玩D&amp；D，但从那时起，这项运动已经发生了很大的变化。沃尔特斯说：“我们玩了第一个，就像坐在你妈妈地下室里的”陌生人的东西“里的东西一样，微缩模型播放的是最嘎吱作响的版本。”
今天，规则变得更简单了，互联网为新玩家和DM提供了无数的资源(包括比如什么是“DM”的词汇表)。
沃尔特斯推荐新玩家去看看D&A；D Beyond。沃尔特斯说：“研发的乐趣之一也是新人最难以抗拒的部分--书太多了。”
但是你不需要任何书就可以开始学习。他将D&amp；D Beyond描述为一套数字工具，从可填写的字符表到规则手册再到数字骰子，应有尽有，应有尽有。
一旦你有了基本的东西，就是组建政党的时候了。你真正需要做的就是找到一群也想玩的人。如果你在现实生活中找不到应聘者，你可以访问一些网站和附属网站。
但是，找到像你这样有着相似的、代表性不足的人口统计和背景的人是很有价值的。
凯莉·林恩·D‘Angelo是“女孩，勇气，荣耀”的编剧和地牢大师，她说，为了一场全女性演出而进行DM-ing是一种新鲜空气。“这是一种完全不同的体验，”D‘Angelo说。“我觉得当我们围坐在桌子旁时，有一种我们都认识到的珍贵。
“我们是口头讲故事的人，我的部落。知识就是这样传递的，信息、文化和我们的生计就是这样传递的，“D‘Angelo说。在许多方面，D&amp；D是另一种口头讲故事的形式。当D‘Angelo第一次与她的全土著政党坐在一起时，她说感觉就像“烟花炸毁了我的心”。
“因为这是我们的祖先所希望的，”D‘Angelo说。“我们的祖先正在庆祝这样一个事实：我们还活着，现在还活着，直到今天，我们还在创造故事。”
如果你觉得和了解你背景的人一起玩会更舒服，那就去找那些人。
人们玩游戏有很多不同的原因-一些人对游戏的战斗和战术更感兴趣，而另一些人可能对角色扮演和讲故事更感兴趣。但是，只要每个人都投入到游戏中和彼此身上，这些不同的利益和方法就可以在正确的桌子上结合起来。
在你开始演奏之前，你必须塑造一个角色。在D&amp；D 5版中，有14个职业，甚至更多的比赛，这取决于你想要获得多大的创造力。把你的角色建立在一个你已经知道并喜欢的虚构角色的基础上并没有错，但这里有一些其他的需要考虑的事情。
选择一个种族和职业，建立你的角色表很重要，但动机也同样重要，也可以帮助你做出这些选择。“维度20”的主持人兼地牢大师布伦南·李·穆利根说，塑造一个完全成型的角色是很诱人的，但你会更成功地塑造一个从参加派对和冒险中获益的角色。
穆利根说：“你能为你的PC(玩家角色)做的最好的事情就是让他们相信这是一个想去冒险的人。”“这是一个在他们的故事中可能有空间的人，可以结交这些新朋友，找到这些其他英雄将要成为的这个新家庭。”
你的角色也可以帮助你在战役之外发现自我。《维度20》的演员艾丽·比尔兹利在《维度20》第一季中饰演一个顺式女人，在第二季中扮演一名变性人(前述的彼特即塞子)，因为他们都在努力寻找自己的性别认同。
比尔兹利说：“过渡对我来说是一件很重要的事情，对我来说是什么样子，我要选择的点菜选择是什么。”“所以说，是的，这是我正在考虑的一个选择，让我们来玩这个游戏，这是一件很有趣的事情。”
比尔兹利故意让他们的角色变性人，但确保这不是他角色弧线的焦点。
“这家伙只是个纽约市的人。他是变性人，这是他现实的一部分，也许他是T，但这不是头条新闻。标题是他看到了魔术，“比尔兹利说。“他的凌日并没有让他黯然失色，这很有趣，因为那是真实的。”
你的角色真的可以是你想要的任何东西，在某种意义上，甚至可以是你，如果你选择的话。
《维度20》的演员卢·威尔逊说：“我个人抓住自己身上让我兴奋地喜欢、看和探索的部分。”“当我塑造角色时，我能像我的角色一样表演出令人兴奋和精彩的表演，因为你们都在看我做我自己。”
然而，威尔逊说，这是有弱点的，尊重这一点也是至关重要的。把自己放在那里可能很有挑战性，因为如果有人取笑你的角色，往往会觉得他们在取笑你。
“但我认为，脆弱真的会带来人们喜欢扮演的最令人兴奋、最全面的角色。”因为一旦你玩了他们，你就会确切地知道你在和什么打交道，“威尔逊说。
威尔逊说，他有过一些最喜欢的职业研发经历，希望自己年轻时就有这样的经历。
威尔逊说：“我真的想鼓励对研发感兴趣或想玩研发的有色人种这样做。”“社会和叙事中有很多东西表明这个游戏不适合我们。但我认为我们在这场游戏中玩得更好，是因为我们从文化的角度讲述了我们自己的故事的深度，以及这些故事在传统的幻想领域中有多少没有表现出来。“。
第一次玩可能会让你伤脑筋，特别是当你不确定会发生什么的时候。但是，如果你和你信任和尊敬的人一起玩，这也可以很好地治愈你的伤痛。当克里斯蒂娜·泰格纳的父亲在4月份去世时，由于新冠肺炎的性格，她正在努力以自己想要的方式哀悼。泰格纳是一名演员和关键角色演员，她说研发部门在她的悲痛过程中起到了帮助作用。泰格纳说：“我发现自己在游戏中有过一些瞬间，我只是在中间崩溃了，就像我和我的角色之间有一条裂缝。”
她说，多年来，她也用D&A；D来解决她的创伤。泰格纳说：“这是一件非常强大的事情，我认为重要的是要知道，这不仅仅是一群书呆子在玩游戏，而是人们在一起玩，感受和联系。”
虽然面对面研发很棒，但泰格纳指出，虚拟游戏也有一些好处。泰格纳说：“你有这张虚拟桌子，我们可以利用它来相互交流，这种亲密性很重要。”
如果你已经掌握了这个游戏的基本知识，或者你就是找不到其他想要做这件事的人，那么可能是时候尝试一下DM了。成为一名地下城高手--如果你玩的是不同的桌面游戏，那就是游戏高手--你必须牢记自己的一套规则和故事。幸运的是，你不是第一个这么做的人。
有很多预先编写好的冒险游戏可以适应你的桌子，还有很多由其他爱好者设计的地图，如果你在网上和朋友一起玩，你可以在Roll20中使用它们。同样重要的是要确保你的球员在桌上感觉舒服。
穆里根建议，让你的角色在第一阶段进入角色，“而不是简单地说，‘好吧，我们要去酒馆，每个人都在做什么’，这对于有点害羞的人来说是一个很好的方式，让他们感觉被蒸汽吞噬了。”
例如，他建议一个场景，在这个场景中，角色处于他们的元素中，或者完成了对他们来说很重要的事情，这样角色背后的玩家从一开始就感觉更有信心。
虽然有一个适当的故事将有助于游戏的发展，但研发是一个协作的努力，你的玩家的投入是非常有价值的。
沃尔特斯说：“当人们感到不堪重负时，他们就会抵制。”“你在那里画场景，让他们有有意义的体验，但在这个过程中，你会有有意义的体验。”你可以看到他们的快乐，当你玩弄他们的时候，他们会把你推向新的、不同的高度。“。
观看诸如“关键角色”、“维度20”、“冒险区”或“女孩，勇气，荣耀”这样的真人秀节目可以为你的故事讲述和作为地下城大师的风格提供灵感。但是不要担心让自己达到专业人士的标准。
“你不能做我们做的事，但我们也不能做你做的事，”沃尔特斯说。“唱你的歌，让你的朋友和同桌的人与你分享那美妙的经历。”

I didn’t really  get Dungeons & Dragons until I came across a video on YouTube titled “ D&D meets NYC.” I had played a few sessions with friends, but I got tripped up at every turn and didn’t think I could ever learn all the rules. As it turned out, “D&D meets NYC” was the first episode of  Dimension20: The Unsleeping City, a recurring actual play D&D show. In the first five minutes, the audience is introduced to Pete the Plug, a sorcerer who is meeting with his doctor shortly after getting top surgery. I was hooked.
 I didn’t realize D&D could look like that—that it didn’t have to exist in the medieval fantasy worlds I had seen and heard so much about. Still, it can be intimidating to break into an activity that’s so dense with jargon and rules and has a history of gatekeeping. WIRED talked to some experts on how to get started and the role of identity in tabletop role-playing games.
  The hardest part, as is true with most things, is getting started. But you really only need to get a couple of friends together and figure it out as you go, says content creator and D&D performer  B. Dave Walters. “Don't stress too much about the rules,” says Walters. “Don't stress too much about what you're supposed to do.” Instead, think of your favorite fantasy movie, show, book, or even character, and go from there.
  “Growing up as a little Black kid in the south, there weren't many opportunities to feel powerful,” says Walters. “And that was one of the things that D&D gave me originally—a chance to feel heroic. Not just reading about superheroes or watching movies, but actually going out and living one.”
  Walters has been playing D&D since he was 13, but the game has changed significantly since then. “We played the first one, like something straight out of  Stranger Things sitting in your mom's basement, with the miniatures playing the crunchiest version of it,” Walters says.
  Today, the rules are simpler and the internet provides new players and DMs with a myriad of resources (including  glossaries on, say, what a “DM” is).
  Walters recommends new players check out  D&D Beyond. “One of the joys of D&D is also one of the most overwhelming parts of it for new people—there's just a lot of books,” Walters says.
 But you don’t need any books to get started. He describes D&D Beyond as a digital tool set that has everything you need to get started, from  fillable character sheets to  rule books to digital dice.
  Once you have the basics down, it’s time to form a party. All you really need to do is find a group of people who also want to play. There are  websites and  subreddits you can tap if you can’t find takers in your real life.
 But it can be valuable to find people from similar, underrepresented demographics and backgrounds like your own.
 Kelly Lynne D’Angelo, writer and dungeon master for   Girls, Guts, Glory, said DM-ing for an all-woman show was a breath of fresh air. “It was a completely different experience,” D’Angelo said. “I felt like when we sat around the table there was sort of a preciousness that we all recognized.
  “We are oral storytellers, my tribe. That's how knowledge is passed on, how information and culture and our livelihood are passed on,” D’Angelo says. And D&D is, in many ways, another form of oral storytelling. When D’Angelo first sat down with her all-Native party, she said it felt like “fireworks exploding my heart.”
  “Because this is what our ancestors would have wanted,” D’Angelo says. “And our ancestors are celebrating the fact that we're alive and present, and we're still creating stories to this day.”
  If you feel like you’d be more comfortable playing with others who understand your background, find those people.
 People play for a lot of different reasons—some are more interested in the combat and tactics of the game, while others may be more interested in the role-playing and storytelling. But those different interests and approaches can coalesce at the right table as long as everyone is invested in the game and in each other.
  Before you start playing, you have to build a character. In D&D 5th Edition, there are  14 classes and even more  races depending on how creative you want to get. There’s nothing wrong with basing your character off a fictional character you already know and love, but here are some other things to consider.
 Picking a race and class, and building your character sheet is important, but motivation is equally important and can also help inform those choices. Brennan Lee Mulligan, host and dungeon master of   Dimension 20, says it can be tempting to create a fully formed character, but you’ll have more success building a character who has something to gain from joining a party and going on an adventure.
 “The best thing you can do for your PC (player character) is build them around the idea that this is a person who wants to go on an adventure,” says Mulligan. “This is a person who maybe has room in their story, to make these new friends and find this new family of who these other heroes are going to be.”
 Your character can also help with self-discovery outside of the campaign.  Dimension 20 cast member Ally Beardsley played a cis woman in the first season of  Dimension 20 and a transgender man (the aforementioned Pete the Plug) in the second, as they worked through their own gender identity.
 “It was a huge thing on my mind of transitioning and what that looks like for me and what of the à la carte options I was going to choose to pursue,” says Beardsley. “So it was fun to be like, yeah, this is an option I'm considering, let's play that.”
 Beardsley intentionally made their character transgender, but made sure that wasn’t the focal point of his character arc.
  “This guy is just a dude in New York City. It is a part of his reality that he's trans, maybe he takes T, but that wasn't the headline. The headline was that he sees magic,” Beardsley says. “His transness doesn’t eclipse him, which was fun because that’s real.”
 Your character can really be anything you want, and it can even be, in some sense,  you, if that’s what you choose.
 “I personally grab onto parts of myself that I'm excited to like, see and explore,” says  Dimension 20 cast member Lou Wilson. “When I create characters, the performances I'm able to give as my characters are exciting and good because you're watching me be me.”
 There is a vulnerability to that, though, Wilson says, and it’s essential to honor that as well. Putting yourself out there can be challenging, because if someone makes fun of your character, it can often feel like they’re making fun of you.
 “But I think that being vulnerable, really, is going to lead to the most exciting, well-rounded characters that people enjoy playing. Because once you are playing them you know exactly what you're working with,” Wilson says.
  Wilson says he has had some of his favorite experiences playing D&D professionally, and wished he had it when he was younger.
 “I really want to encourage people of color who are interested in or thought about playing D&D to do so,” Wilson said. “There's a lot in society and in the narrative that suggests that this game is not for us. But I think we play this game better because of the depth of our own narratives that we carry from a cultural sense and how much those aren't represented in the traditional fantasy realms.”
  Your first time playing can be nerve-wracking, especially when you aren’t sure what to expect. But if you’re playing with people you trust and respect, it can also be very healing. When Krystina Tigner’s father passed away in April, she was struggling to grieve in the way she wanted, due to the nature of Covid-19. Tigner, an actor and Critical Role cast member, says that D&D was helpful in her grieving process. “I found myself having moments in-game where I just broke down in the middle, like there was this crack between myself and my character,” Tigner said.
  Over the years, she says, she’s used D&D to work through her trauma as well. “It's a very powerful thing, and I think it's important to know that it's not just a bunch of nerds playing a game, but it is people playing together and feeling and connecting,” Tigner says.
 While in-person D&D is great, Tigner notes there are some benefits to virtual gameplay as well. “You've got this virtual table that we can take advantage of, to interface with each other, and that closeness is important,” says Tigner.
  If you’ve gotten the basics of the game down, or you just can’t find anyone else who wants to do it, it may be time to try your hand at DM-ing. Being a dungeon master—or game master if you’re playing a different tabletop game—comes with its own set of rules and stories you have to keep in your head. Luckily, you’re not the first one to do it.
 There are plenty of  prewritten adventures that you can adapt to your table, and plenty of  maps designed by other enthusiasts that you can use in  Roll20 if you’re playing with friends virtually. It’s also important to make sure that your players feel comfortable at the table.
 Mulligan suggests giving your characters scenes of their own to get into character during the first session, “rather than just being like, ‘OK, we're going to Tavern, what's everybody doing,’ which is a great way for someone who's a little bit shyer to feel steamrolled.”
 For example, he suggests a scene where the character is in their element, or accomplishing something important to them, so the player behind the character feels more confident from the beginning.
 And while having a story in place will help move the game along, D&D is a collaborative effort, and your players’ input is extremely valuable.
 “When people feel railroaded, they will resist it,” says Walters. “You're there to paint the scene for them to have a meaningful experience, but in the process,  you get to have meaningful experiences. You get to see their joy, and as you're playing off of them, they will push you to new and different heights.”
  Watching actual play shows like   Critical Role,   Dimension 20,   The Adventure Zone, or   Girls, Guts, Glory can provide inspiration for your storytelling and your style as a dungeon master. But don’t worry about holding yourself to the standard of the professionals.
 “You can't do what we do, but we also can't do what you do,” Walters says. “Sing your song, and let your friends and people at your table share that wonderful experience with you.”
